# Heliosphere

> Procedural planet generator — free, browser-based, no signup required.

Heliosphere generates realistic procedural planets shaped by tectonic plate simulation, erosion, and climate modeling. It runs entirely in the browser using Three.js and requires no installation, account, or payment.

## What it does

- Generates unique terrestrial and alien planets with realistic continents, mountains, ocean trenches, and volcanic islands
- Simulates tectonic plates with convergent, divergent, and transform boundaries
- Applies glacial, hydraulic, and thermal erosion to carve fjords, river valleys, and talus slopes; computes flow accumulation to render river corridors as blue tinting in terrain and satellite views
- Stamps procedural impact craters on airless and trace-atmosphere worlds: crater geometry scales to actual world radius (not Earth-hardcoded), power-law size distribution from micro-craters to mega-basins, density scales with 1/worldSize so small bodies are more heavily cratered, tier radii capped at 55% of world radius. Regolith gardening flattens ancient tectonic mountains on airless worlds; tectonic relief suppression on tiny bodies (worldSize < 0.4) makes crater rims the dominant topography as on Phobos
- Simulates seasonal climate: wind patterns, ocean currents, precipitation, and alien-aware Köppen classification (30 standard Earth types + 5 alien zones: Cryo-Desert, Deep Freeze, Primordial, Scorched, Hellscape — activated when planetary temperatures fall outside Earth's range)
- Creates hotspot volcanism with drift-trail island chains (like Hawaii)
- Allows interactive editing — click any tectonic plate to reshape continents in real time
- Supports a full Planetary Physics layer — gravity, world size, atmosphere, hydrosphere, base temperature, and axial tilt flow through the entire simulation pipeline. Nine world presets (Earth-like, Mars-like, Venus-like, Titan-like, Dead Rock, Ocean World, Ice Ball, Arid Desert, High Gravity, or Random Alien) produce genuinely alien worlds with adapted biome colors, atmosphere glow, atmospheric haze sphere, and fluid behavior
- Includes a Solar System Mode (⊙ System button): a 2D top-down real-time orrery powered by Kepler orbital mechanics. Browse our Solar System (Sun through Neptune, including Earth's Moon, Phobos, and Deimos) or generate a random procedural alien system with 0–2 moons per rocky planet. Click any rocky, icy, or moon body to generate its full globe. When viewing a planet its moons orbit visibly in 3D with orbit rings and floating name labels; orbit speed is visually compressed so movement is always perceptible; click targets are generously sized. Click a moon to drill in; click the parent planet disc to return. Entering any body triggers a smooth camera fly-in from deep space. Bodies are queued for silent background generation; a game clock with 1×/10×/100×/1000× time compression drives orbital animation.

## Who it's for

- Worldbuilders creating fantasy or sci-fi settings
- Game developers who need heightmaps for Unity, Unreal, or other engines
- Tabletop RPG players building campaign worlds (D&D, Pathfinder, etc.)
- Fantasy/sci-fi authors designing believable planets
- Artists and hobbyists who enjoy procedural generation
- Educators teaching plate tectonics and planetary science

## Key capabilities

- Multiple views: terrain, satellite biome, Köppen climate, heightmap
- 28 detailed inspection layers (geology, atmosphere, ocean, climate, planetary)
- Köppen climate classification is alien-temperature-aware: temperature decode uses the planet's actual thermal range, so ice worlds (-100°C base temp) never show hot deserts, Venus-class worlds show Hellscape coverage, and hot+wet worlds show Primordial coverage
- Export high-resolution equirectangular maps up to 65,536px wide
- Shareable planet codes — copy a compact code to reproduce any planet exactly
- Solar System Mode: 2D Keplerian orrery for our solar system or a procedural alien system; click any rocky/icy body to view its full generated globe; background queue pre-generates all worlds
- Works on desktop and mobile browsers
- No build step, no dependencies to install — pure ES modules

## URL

https://orogen.studio/

## Technical details

- Built with Three.js (WebGL), vanilla JavaScript ES modules
- Fibonacci sphere meshing with Voronoi tessellation
- Braun-Willett stream power erosion, Barnes et al. pit-filling algorithm
- Dual-model precipitation (advection simulation + zonal heuristic)
- Fully client-side — no server, no data collection
